revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]][[ja:網野銚子山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Amino Chōshiyama Kofun | native_name =網野銚子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Amino Choshiyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Amino Chōshiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Amino Chōshiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.678056|N|135.029722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|41.20|N|135|01|48.00|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}}
The {{nihongo|'''Amino Chōshiyama Kofun'''|網野銚子山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Amino neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1637 |title=銚子山古墳 |trans-title= Chōshiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Amino Chōshiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at the mouth of the Fukuta River that flows through the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] into the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middleof the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The surrounding area has a number of {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} and [[Kofun period]] remains. The tumulus is orientated to the north-northeast and has an overall length of 201 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|qid=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}} region have been found. In addition, the tumulus is surrounded by incomplete moats with a width of 17 to 25 meters on east, south and west sides. As no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted, the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middleof the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}} region.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Amino Station|en|Amino Station|qid=Q4746427|lt=Amino Station|1=Amino Station}} on the {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|qid=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line|1=Miyazu Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The National Historic Site designation includes the smaller circular "Choshiyama Kofun No. 1 and No. 2 Kofun" nearby.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Total length: 201 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 80 meters wide x 10 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 16 meters high, 3-tiers ==Gallery== <gallery> Amino Choshiyama Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg|Panoramic view 網野銚子山古墳出土 埴輪群.JPG|Haniwa from the Amino Chōshiyama Kofun Amino Choshiyama Kofun, baizuka-1.jpg|Choshiyama Kofun No. 1 Amino Choshiyama Kofun, baizuka-2.jpg|Choshiyama Kofun No. 2 </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline}} *[https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/11097/ Kyōtango Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3301.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
